content: Protein binding is measured by equilibrium dialysis or ultrafiltration, separating free from bound drug. Fraction bound = (Total - Free) / Total × 100%. Normal albumin: 3.5-5.0 g/dL; each 1 g/dL decrease reduces binding capacity by ~20%. Alpha-1-acid glycoprotein is an acute-phase reactant, increasing in inflammation, trauma, MI - increasing binding of basic drugs (lidocaine, propranolol). Binding affinity expressed as dissociation constant (Kd); lower Kd indicates tighter binding. Non-linear binding occurs when therapeutic concentrations approach protein binding capacity (saturation kinetics). Temperature and pH affect binding; acidosis decreases albumin binding. In vitro artifact: sample handling can alter measured binding if temperature changes.
